content.name: Barcelona in One Day: Sagrada Familia, Park Güell and the Old Town - Walking Tour with Hotel Pick-up
importantInfo: Children must be accompanied by an adult.

meetingPoint.description: Barcelona in One Day: Sagrada Familia, Park Güell and the Old Town - Walking Tour with Hotel Pick-up

comments.description: Printed voucher or E-voucher. Print and bring the voucher or show the voucher on your mobile device to enjoy the activity.

content.description:
Start your tour in a comfy vehicle that will come to pick you up at your hotel. Enjoy an 8-hours tour which includes a complete visit of the best of Barcelona, such as the Passeig de Gràcia, an elegant boulevard that hosts a wealth of modernist architecture like La Pedrera and Casa Batlló. Merge yourself in a dream world with Gaudi’s masterpieces, visit the astonishing Sagrada Familia and the wonderful Park Guell.

Explore the incredible history of Barcelona’s Gothic Quarter in a walking tour of the Old Town, drive to the beach to have a look to the Old Port and the Barceloneta neighbourhood and finish with great views of Barcelona from the top of the Montjuic hill.

Highlights
• Sample the best of Barcelona on this full-day sightseeing tour
• Enjoy breathtaking panoramic views of the city, and tour the 1992 Olympic Village located on top of Montuïc Mountain
• Avoid long lines at La Sagrada Familia, Gaudí’s crowning masterpiece
• Enjoy a guided tour inside the Sagrada Familia
• Wander along the ancient Roman walls and narrow, winding streets of the Gothic Quarter before visiting a 2,000-year-old Roman Temple
• Gain priority access to Park Güell, a beautifully landscaped park decorated with Gaudí's works
• Stroll down the elegant Passeig de Gracia, marveling at Modernist masterpieces like La Pedrera and Casa Batlló
• Get a taste of the Mediterranean lifestyle and enjoy Barcelona’s coastline through the city’s old fishing district and modern-day beach Platja La Barceloneta

Experience the different faces of Barcelona in this thorough and complete tour to the main highlights of the city with our expert guide that will share fascinating stories about the city’s one-of-a-kind architecture, culture and history.

Start your tour from your hotel, our guide will come to pick you up in a comfy minivan to start your tour. Pass by Barcelona’s main highlights, have a look to the Plaça Catalunya, Barcelona’s central square, that connects to the avenue Passeig de Grácia, an elegant boulevard that hosts a wealth of modernist architecture like the famous Gaudi’s Casa Batlló and La Pedrera, but also the amazing Casa Ametller and Casa Lleo i Morera.

Continue your visit with your expert guide to the modernist area of the city heading to the bohemian Gràcia district, all the way to Park Güell. This magical park is located on the top of a hill that provides spectacular views of Barcelona. Merge yourself in Gaudi’s dream world, walk into unique architectural structures that perfectly fuse with natural elements, designed to leave a lifelong impression. Your guide will be the perfect host during your journey inside the Park.

Pass by the old Hospital de la Santa Creu i Sant Pau, declared a UNESCO World Heritage for its exceptional beauty and functionality, explore the Eixample district following the footsteps of Gaudi to visit a true gem of human heritage, the Sagrada Familia.

The work of a genius or of a fool, this indisputable masterpiece will certainly be the highlight of your visit to Barcelona. Take your time to visit the peculiar shape of the columns, ceilings, the organic architecture of the basilica and the many examples of religious symbolism hidden in its unique façades.

Continue your day passing by the Agbar Tower, in the heart of the economic district of Barcelona, and by the Plaza de Toros Monumental on the way to the seaside.
Enjoy the refreshing breeze while you have a look at the Olympic Village and the Olympic Port, the result of Barcelona’s 1992 Olympic Games. Take a picture to the Golden Fish by Frank Gehry at the feet of the Arts Hotel, one of the most famous skyscrapers in town.

The visit to the Olympic Barcelona continues to the top of the Montjuic Hill, where you will admire the Olympic Stadium and the Sant Jordi Palace, as well as the famous Calatrava’s Communication Tower with your expert guide that will answer all of your questions.

Pass by Plaza España, designed for the 1929 International Exhibition and inspired by the Vatican’s Saint Peter Square. Have a look at the Magic Fountain before heading to the Old Town.

Reach the Gothic Quarter to discover a maze of winding streets dating back to Roman times. Relax in intimate squares and visit medieval Barcelona with its charming Gothic Cathedral, have a look to Sant Jaume Square, the political heart of Barcelona since Romans times and nowadays flanked by Barcelona's two political powerhouses: Barcelona City Hall and the seat of the Catalan government, the Palau de la Generalitat de Catalunya.

raw.name: Barcelona in One Day: Sagrada Familia, Park Güell and the Old Town - Walking Tour with Hotel Pick-up

options.name: Sagrada Familia and Montserrat Full Day Tour with Hotel Pick-up

content.name: Sagrada Familia and Montserrat Full Day Tour with Hotel Pick-up
importantInfo: Children must be accompanied by an adult. </br> Please make sure you answer all the mandatory questions during the booking process.
content.description:
This great value all-in-one tour will allow you to discover Barcelona and Montserrat to gain a deep understanding of Catalonia in just a day. Your guide will pick you up at your accommodation at Barcelona in a comfortable vehicle. During the tour, the guide will share interesting stories about Catalonia, Barcelona, and Montserrat, giving you a deep understanding of the area while keeping the tour entertaining and engaging throughout the day.

Highlights
• Discover the hilltop religious retreat of Montserrat and sample the best of Barcelona
• Visit the 12th-century carving of Our Lady of Montserrat, also known as La Moreneta.
• Explore the 'Holy Cave' where the Madonna is said to have once appeared.
• Listen to La Escolania, one of the oldest children’s choirs in all of Europe (if available).
• Enjoy breathtaking panoramic views of the city
• Avoid long lines at La Sagrada Familia, Gaudí’s crowning masterpiece.
• Wander along the ancient Roman walls and narrow, winding streets of the Gothic Quarter before visiting a 2,000-year-old Roman Temple.
• Stroll down the elegant Passeig de Gracia, marveling at Modernist masterpieces like La Pedrera and Casa Batlló.

Enjoy convenient pickup at your Barcelona accommodation. Walk on the street's intricately paved stonework, designed by the legendary Antoni Gaudi. Shop at one of the many boutiques that line the Passeig de Gracia boulevard and admire the striking facades of the modernist buildings while listening to your guide's commentary. Snap photos of architectural masterpieces like La Pedrera, Casa Batllo, and Casa Lleo.

You'll also see the Eixample, designed by Ildefons Cerda. Pass the Hospital de Sant Pau as you make your way to La Sagrada Familia. Learn about the different parts of the building, their symbolism and the designer that created them.

Continue to the Ciutat Vella neighborhood. Stop at Arc de Triomf and the Parc de la Ciutadella, an old fortress that is now a city park. Admire the Castle of the Three Dragons or the Grief, a sculpture by the lake before walking down La Rambla to the new World Trade Center.

Next, climb Montjuic for spectacular panoramic views. See the facilities from the 1992 Olympics, Palau Sant Jordi, and the "urban-ism" designed for the International Exhibition of 1929.
Drive 60 km northwest of Barcelona, where Montserrat stands majestically - up to 4000 feet in height. The look of this rock mass, known for the whimsical shapes of its peaks soaring through the sky, is the result of a process of erosion over millions of years. In its ascent, you will discover the most mysteriously beautiful views of Catalonia.

In the mountain, 2380 feet above sea level and surrounded by this magnificent scenery, is located the Benedictine monastery of Santa Maria de Montserrat. It is also where you will find the Virgin of Montserrat - the "Black Madonna". According to legend, the virgin appeared in the IX century, giving rise the hermitage of Santa Maria and its worship for centuries. Patron and symbol of Catalonia, "Black Madonna" has been venerated into a XII century Romanesque carving which is characterized by its unique dark skin, hence the nickname as it is popularly known. After visiting the monastery, a walking tour of its surroundings will allow us to enjoy the unparalleled views of this privileged environment.

raw.name: Sagrada Familia and Montserrat Full Day Tour with Hotel Pick-up

options.name: La Pedrera Essential - Ticket

content.name: La Pedrera Essential - Ticket
importantInfo: Valid photo ID required. </br> Students will need to bring the correct documentation to show at the entrance.</br> Children under 6 years old are free of charge.</br> Children aged 13 or older pay the adult price.
content.description:
Casa Milà, popularly known as ‘La Pedrera’, is a most unusual building, constructed between 1906 and 1912 by the architect Antoni Gaudí (1852–1926) and declared UNESCO World Heritage in 1984. Today it is the headquarters of Fundació Catalunya-La Pedrera and houses a cultural center that is a reference point in Barcelona for the range of activities it organizes and the different spaces for exhibitions and other public uses it contains. La Pedrera today is a beacon shining with creation and knowledge, a great container full of content, which has a crucial role to play in the transformation of society and commitment to the people.

La Pedrera is probably one of the most famous buildings of the Modernista or Catalan Art Nouveau period and one of the architects Antoni Gaudí’s most ambitious works. It is a container that is a work of art in itself.

As a cultural center, La Pedrera hosts outstanding art exhibitions and debates that connect with present and future lines of thought. A learning space La Pedrera is a place where knowledge and learning are the keys to the construction of a rich, dynamic future society.

La Pedrera offers accessible resources and services in its spaces and amenities.

Languages
Audio guide available in Catalan, Spanish, English, French, German, Portuguese, Italian, Russian, Chinese, Japanese, and Korean
content.summary: One of Antoni Gaudí’s most ambitious works, La Pedrera is a fascinating reference point for culture in Barcelona.
highligths: One of the most famous buildings from the Modernista or Catalan Art Nouveau period
Get to know the work of Gaudí in detail

raw.name: La Pedrera Essential - Ticket

options.name: Sagrada Familia Guided Tour

content.name: Sagrada Familia Guided Tour
importantInfo: Children must be accompanied by an adult. </br> Please make sure you answer all the mandatory questions during the booking process.
content.description:
Join us on a comprehensive guided tour of Gaudi´s world-famous masterpiece, La Sagrada Familia. On this tour, you will be able to skip the lines and visit Sagrada Familia with an enthusiastic expert guide. You will see the different parts of the building and the many examples of religious symbolism hidden through its unique architecture.

Highlights
• Avoid long lines at La Sagrada Familia, Gaudí’s crowning masterpiece
• Discover the facades of Sagrada Familia and learn about its symbolism
• Enter the monument with your guide for an essential visit with an expert
• Learn about the world-famous architect Antoni Gaudí

Explore the most famous masterpiece of Catalan architect Antoni Gaudí, La Sagrada Familia. Discover this masterpiece together with an expert guide. Your guide will be waiting for you next to Sagrada Familia, at the corner of Carrer Sardenya and Carrer de Provença.

From the meeting point first, walk around the basilica with your guide and visit its facades. The first one you will be able to see is the Passion Facade, which was the last one to be built. After visiting the newest facade and going around Sagrada Familia you will get to see the Nativity Facade. This facade was built under the direct supervision of Gaudí and serves as the entrance to the church. You will learn about the many examples of religious symbolism of these intricate facades as well as the history of the church and its architect.

After seeing the Sagrada Familia from the outside you will enter the church with your guide. We offer the exclusive opportunity to skip the line, in order to save precious time. Entering the monument with an expert guide only for you will allow you to have a personalized experience and take full advantage of your visit.

You are sure to be captivated by Gaudi’s incredible architectural style: the peculiar shape of the columns, ceilings, walls and the organic architecture of the basilica.

raw.name: Sagrada Familia Guided Tour

options.name: Private Game of Thrones: Medieval Girona in Half-Day Tour with Hotel pick-up (HD)

content.name: Private Game of Thrones: Medieval Girona in Half-Day Tour with Hotel pick-up (HD)
content.description:
Join this walking tour of Girona Old Town that will lead you to the hidden spots where Game of Thrones was filmed. Our passionate tour guide will come to pick you up at your hotel or apartment in Barcelona to start your adventure. Busses are forbidden to enter the city walls, but we are able to bring you at the heart of the Old Town, since our transport is a private minivan or executive car.

With its perfect location on a hill along the eastern shore of the Onyar River and near the French border, after the Roman settlement and during medieval times, Girona flourished into the perfect spot for commercial and cultural exchanges.

Walking through Girona Old Town is like traveling back in time since all the footprints of its glorious past are clearly on sight. That is why it has been chosen as one of the best settings for the filming of many scenes of Game of Thrones HBO series.

Our expert guide will show you the most magical corners of the city, among them the places that have brought Game of Thrones to life. You will feel like Arya Stark in the streets of Braavos. You can’t miss the chance to take a picture, sitting on the magnificent Iron Throne of the series.

Have a look to the unmissable Girona Cathedral, famous for having the world’s widest Gothic nave and nowadays well renown also for representing the Great Sept of Baelor, where Cersei Lannister caused the terrific explosion with the green fiery blaze, in the sixth season of Game of Thrones. You will also recognize the monumental baroque stairway of the Cathedral, used as the exterior of Great Sept Baelor in King’s Landing where Jaime Lannister has showdown with the High Sparrow.

Walk through the Jewish Quarter, where a major part of the scenes has been recorded, representing the city of Braavos, where blind Ayra Stark was begging and got attacked by the Waif.

Enjoy great views from the Medieval City Walls and from Gustave Eiffel’s bridge of 1877. Discover the Arabic baths in the Old Town as well as the synagogue.

Stroll through key filming sites of Game of Thrones and get lost in a maze of winding streets. Walk into Medieval history and create your own Game of Thrones story. Return with a heart full of memories.

raw.name: Private Game of Thrones: Medieval Girona in Half-Day Tour with Hotel pick-up (HD)

options.name: Private Costa Brava: Tossa and Panoramic Boat Ride Full-Day Tour with Hotel Pick-Up (FD)

content.name: Private Costa Brava: Tossa and Panoramic Boat Ride Full-Day Tour with Hotel Pick-Up (FD)
content.description:
Visit the Costa Brava and discover its hidden and natural coves and its crystal clear waters that bathe the shore of towns full of history and charm.

Our professional guide will pick you up in your hotel in a private vehicle and, together with a small friendly group of no more than 8 people will drive you to Costa Brava. There you will visit its most beautiful and distinctive fishing towns surrounded by the brightest greens as well as the beautiful turquoise blue of the sea.

You will first go to Lloret de Mar, where you will take a ship that will take you to the next destination in a very pleasant route. In this route you will discover different idyllic coves hidden in the coastline. During the tour, you will be able to relax and enjoy the different colors in the landscape, the salty marine breeze and the shiny water, as well as the amazing marine life of Costa Brava, which you will observe through the submarine vision of the ship.

Your next destination is Tossa de Mar, an old fishing village chosen as a summer residence by great artists and intellectuals. In order to discover this town full of history, you will go up to its medieval castle, where you will relish in a spectacular view. Then you will walk across the streets in the Vila Vella quarter, where you will see the structure of the fortified medieval town. After visiting the town, you will be able to taste the typical cuisine of Costa Brava, based on the delicious fresh fish of the area.

Going to the next visit, our guide will drive you through a panoramic road on a journey with spectacular views of the sea and nature, this is only possible in small groups as driving a bus through that road can be difficult. During the route, you will stop to take some amazing photos of the coast. Our expert guide will choose one of the most beautiful beaches of Spain, where you will be able to dive in the crystalline Mediterranean waters surrounded by nature, as well as getting to enjoy the typical idyllic calm that defines the coves of Costa Brava.

raw.name: Private Costa Brava: Tossa and Panoramic Boat Ride Full-Day Tour with Hotel Pick-Up (FD)

options.name: Private Cava and Vineyards Half-Day Tour with Hotel pick-up from Barcelona (HD)

content.name: Private Cava and Vineyards Half-Day Tour with Hotel pick-up from Barcelona (HD)
content.description:
Round-trip hotel pick-up & drop-off included.

Enjoy this half-day tour out of the crowd of the city in a private tour that brings you to the verdant vineyards of the Penedès region. At the foot of the Montserrat Mountain, visit a family run vineyards that has been cultivating grapes since 1385 and a world leader in the Cava production.

Learn about the rich wine tradition of this area from your passionate guide. Descent into atmospheric wine cellars to get to know about the vineyard growth cycles as well as organic grape growing, surrounded by unbeatable views over the Penedès wine region and the Montserrat mountain range.

Try 3 Cava Reserva sparkling wines carefully selected and perfectly paired with local cured meats and cheeses.

Start your Private journey to the discovery of Catalonia’s fertile soils and warm climate, the perfect combination for rich wine production.

Head to the Penedès region, located at the foot of the Montserrat Mountain, cradling verdant vineyards.

Enjoy the beautiful green and mountainous scenery, while driving through and get to know Sant Sadurní d’Anoia, the capital of Cava.

We bring you to a family-run cellar of 1385, celebrated for its organic and ecological cultivation of grapes. The cellar is well known for its premium production of a superior Cava called Corpinnat, a distinguished great sparkling wine, made in the heart of the Penedès from 100% organic grapes harvested by hand and entirely vinified on the premises of the winery.

Enjoy a professional Cava tasting, paired with some local Tapas.

Visit the cellar and descent to the dungeons where the wine is [redacted]ly kept for several years. Learn by the hand of an expert viticultor on wine production and grapes growing.

Taste 3 varieties of local wines and cavas, paired with traditional cheeses and cold meats while enjoying great views of the Catalan countryside with the Montserrat mountain at the background.

raw.name: Private Cava and Vineyards Half-Day Tour with Hotel pick-up from Barcelona (HD)

options.name: [redacted]s of the Gothic Quarter - Private Tour

content.name: [redacted]s of the Gothic Quarter - Private Tour
importantInfo: Please make sure you answer all the mandatory questions during the booking process.
content.description:
Barcelona is one of the largest and most famous old cities in the world, and this tour is undoubtedly a unique experience to get lost in its labyrinthine streets.

Begin the tour at the Arc de Triomf, passing through the Parque de la Ciutadella, one of the locals favourite places to relax, and home to the Catalonian Parliament, the Zoo and even an impressive waterfall, to name but a few!

From here, enter the oldest districts in the city, with over 2000 years of history, and which still preserves hidden corners, monuments or shops that date back centuries! Take a stroll through Plaza Sant Jaume, where the Government and City Hall, the Jewish quarter, the Cathedral, Santa María del Mar and Paseo del Born, amongst others are located, The Gothic Quarter is a unique place that undoubtedly captivates everyone's hearts!

raw.name: [redacted]s of the Gothic Quarter - Private Tour

options.name: Panoramic Montjuic Mountain - Private Tour

content.name: Panoramic Montjuic Mountain - Private Tour
content.description:
Once the tour guide meets you at your hotel or apartment, the tour through Montjuic hill will start by climbing the mountain with the cable car from where you will enjoy the spectacular views of the city. When we get to the top of the hill we will visit the old fortress which was used with military purposes to defend Barcelona from enemies.

We will descend the mountain and visit the area where the Olympics took place in 1992. We will have the chance to visit the major facilities of this amazing complex including the Olympic Stadium, the Palau Sant Jordi and the Telecommunications Tower.

The next stop on our tour will be the magnificent National Museum of Catalan Art, a spectacular building where we will visit its outstanding dome. Just before our tour ends we will explore the Plaza España which is one of the most popular squares in the city and contains many impressive monuments.

raw.name: Panoramic Montjuic Mountain - Private Tour

options.name: Montserrat Mountain -  Private Tour with Lunch Included

content.name: Montserrat Mountain -  Private Tour with Lunch Included
content.description:
Your private tour guide will meet you at your hotel or apartment to start off your experience, then you will head to the train station by public transport, where we will take the train and enjoy the Catalonian landscape on our way to Monistrol, located at the bottom of the mountain. Once there you can choose between the rack train or the cable car to get to the popular Santa María de Montserrat Abbey. From this viewpoint you will be able to take amazing pictures.

We will visit the Basilica and we will visit the Virgin of Montserrat, known as Black Madonna “La Moreneta”, a wood carving from the twelfth century, which is the patron saint of Catalonia.

After this experience we can do some hiking to an amazing viewpoint located about 25 minutes away from the monastery or watch an audiovisual to found out about the life of the monks. At the end of this experience we will have lunch to recharge our batteries and return to Barcelona.

For those who love nature and culture, this will be a great experience, for sure!

Lunch and drinks:
if you opt for the hiking experience we will buy some sandwiches to eat on the train back, if not, we will have lunch in the monastery’s restaurant.

raw.name: Montserrat Mountain -  Private Tour with Lunch Included

options.name: Barcelona Jewish Quarter - Private Tour

content.name: Barcelona Jewish Quarter - Private Tour
importantInfo: Please indicate the name of the hotel at the moment of making the reservation, otherwise the booking will not be considered confirmed. </br>
content.description:
On this 1.5-hour tour you will have the opportunity to learn about the history of Barcelona’s Jewish community and uncover a part of its past: What was life like for the Jewish community in Barcelona? When did they settle in the city? What became of them?

You will visit the Call Mayor and the Call Menor (the areas which make up Barcelona’s Jewish Quarter) and find out what role they played in Barcelona’s history, as well as discover the city’s most prominent ancient monuments related to the history of the Jewish community: The Ancient Roman Wall, the Ancient Synagogue of Barcelona and La Plaza del Rey (“King’s Square”) found in the Jewish Quarter.

You will also have the chance to unearth those vestiges of Barcelona’s Jewish history which remain hidden and scattered among the squares and alleys of Old Town Barcelona.

raw.name: Barcelona Jewish Quarter - Private Tour

options.name: Spanish Tapas like a local - Private Tour

content.name: Spanish Tapas like a local - Private Tour
content.description:
Experience a fantastic day while you get to taste some of the exquisite tapas (appetizer) and wines in this 3-hour tour.

In the popular neighborhoods, we will find the best and most typical bars of the area. We will visit 3 bars, each of them portraying its own style and personality.

Our expert local guide will give you advice in these bars, where you will taste different snacks, from Spanish tapas, or typical tapas from the north of Spain, to delicious haute cuisine snacks.

Relax and enjoy this tradition while we talk about the history, culture and life style of this amazing city.

Good food lovers will enjoy this tour, that’s for sure!

raw.name: Spanish Tapas like a local - Private Tour

options.name: F. C. Barcelona: Immersive Private Tour

content.name: F. C. Barcelona: Immersive Private Tour
importantInfo: Please make sure you answer all the mandatory questions during the booking process.</br>
content.description:
If you like football and sports, FC Barcelona and its spectacular stadium, Camp Nou is definitely something that you should visit in this city.


Our guide, who is a big Barça fan, will be with you during the visit to the gigantic sports stadium of FC Barcelona. We will have the opportunity to see some facilities like the Palau Blaugrana, the Mini Estadi, the skating rink or the world’s largest Barça store.


We will go inside the museum and the Multimedia Zone and we will see the majestic stadium, Camp Nou, from the outside and the inside, visiting different places like the presidential area, the visitor locker rooms, the press room and, eventually, you will be able to stand in front of the field, where we will relive the best moments of one of the best football team in the world: the Barça.


After touring an exhibition full of emotional content, we will access the new space "Spotify Camp Nou Live", where we will enjoy an immersive large-format 360º show. You will be the protagonist of the show, and as if you were in the center of the stadium, you will be able to experience from a unique point of view the emotions that represent what has been, is, and will be the largest stadium in Europe.


To end the tour, we will visit a complete exhibition of the Espai Barça architectural project, where there are models and audiovisual pieces to present and discover all the details of what will be the new home of all Barcelona fans.


You’ll be able to live a unique experience and discover all the [redacted]s of FC Barcelona with us!


Note

The virtual reality experience at the Museum is not included.


Important Information - There may be restrictions

Access to the Spotify Camp Nou will be restricted due to ongoing construction, and as a result, the FC Barcelona Museum will be the only area open to visitors. The stadium will remain closed and will not be available for visits.


Schedule

Closing dates: 1st of January, 24th of June, 11th of September, 25th of December, 26th of December, and the match days.


Languages

English and Spanish.

raw.name: F. C. Barcelona: Immersive Private Tour

options.name: Private Tarragona and Sitges Full-Day Tour

content.name: Private Tarragona and Sitges Full-Day Tour
importantInfo: Please make sure you answer all the mandatory questions during the booking process.</br> Children must be accompanied by an adult.
content.description:
Discover Tarragona, located 62 miles (100 km) south of Barcelona, on the Costa Dorada.


Highlights
- Full-day tour to Sitges and Tarragona from Barcelona
- Visit two gems of Catalonia's Costa Dorada
- Explore the UNESCO-listed ancient Roman ruins of Tarragona
- Soak up the artistic atmosphere of Sitges on a walking tour
- Relax or swim at beautiful San Sebastian urban beach
- Private tour to ensure a personalized attention
- Hotel pickup and drop-off included

The city is home to one of the most important and best preserved Roman archaeological sites in Spain, declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. Next, we'll begin the walking tour, where you will discover the many still preserved remains of this civilization, while our guide provides their most exciting stories.
You will be able to explore the ancient walls surrounding the city and be immersed into one of the best preserved circuses in Europe, hidden under building structures from the 19th century. You will see as the traces of the ancient Roman forum live in the present with the medieval and modern Tarragona. Finally, you will be amazed at the ruins of the amphitheater located on the shore of the Mediterranean since the 2nd century and where bloody battles between gladiators took place.

After this journey in the footsteps of our distant past, we'll go to another town with a charm that is more colorful and idyllic, heir to the imprint of Modernism.
Sitges is located 23.6 miles (38 km) from Barcelona. Its privileged location, where the sea and the mountains are mixed, became an ideal place to practice health tourism. Today, in Sitges, is a mixture of traditional fishermen’s houses with a bohemian and lively atmosphere, full of boutiques, restaurants and musicians and artists in the streets.
On a walking tour through the town, your guide will take you through the wonderful streets and the most hidden corners of its labyrinthine center. You will see examples of architectural modernism of Casa Bacardi or Casa del Rellotge, as you uncover the curious stories of the 'Americanos’ who became rich from the new continent. Sitges is an idyllic walk by the sea, including the church of San Bartolomé and Santa Tecla, the grand staircase, Maricel Palace and the charming ‘Quiet Corner’, where musicians enliven the atmosphere, forming a beautiful journey that leads to the beach of San Sebastian.
Relax in one of its terraces or, if the weather permits, take a dip in its crystal waters.

Languages
English

raw.name: Private Tarragona and Sitges Full-Day Tour

options.name: Besalú and Medieval Towns - Full-Day Private Tour

content.name: Besalú and Medieval Towns - Full-Day Private Tour
importantInfo: Please make sure you answer all the mandatory questions during the booking process.</br> Children must be accompanied by an adult.
content.description:
Higlights

• Discover the gems of the city of Vic, with its old town, its cathedral and its markets.

• Take a walk and discover the beautiful medieval town of Santa Pau.

• Have a spectacular view of Castellfollit de la Roca and take a beautiful picture.

• Enjoy the wildest natural landscape of la Garrotxa, as well as its unique light.

• Visit Besalú, the most spectacular medieval town of Catalunya.



Pass by Barcelona’s main highlights, have a look to the Plaça Catalunya, Barcelona’s central square, that connects to the avenue Passeig de Grácia, an elegant boulevard that hosts a wealth of modernist architecture like the famous Gaudi’s Casa Batlló and La Pedrera, but also the amazing Casa Ametller and Casa Lleo i Morera.

Continue the journey heading to Vic, a town that offers visitors one of the most memorable medieval settings in all Catalonia region. Due to the town’s destruction and reconstruction after the Saracen invasion in the 9th Century, Vic has been completely rebuilt. A whole new city grew around some of Catalunya’s most important landmarks, including the Episcopal Museum, Cathedral and Main Square (also home of a popular weekly market). Get to know important corners of the medieval city, including the paintings of Josep Maria Sert in the Cathedral, and the Roman temple. Discover the history and hidden [redacted]s of these wonderful relics along the way.

After the walking tour and before to head to the next destination, savor authentic Catalan cuisine for lunch in one of the best places, recommended by your guide.


Continue your tour crossing la Garrotxa and observing volcanoes and other magnificent geological structures, before arriving to Santa Pau. Enjoy a walking tour through one of the most charming towns of Historic Catalunya, in the company of your guide and take the chance to walk down along the beautiful medieval streets of this town, visiting the main square.


Stop at the foot of Castellfollit de la Roca, a spectacular medieval town that hangs from the edge of a basalt cliff. This is a great place to take some photos while enjoying the view.

End your back-in-time journey at the final and most important stop of the tour, Besalú. With its incredibly rich heritage and wide array of monuments, Besalú offers visitors one of the best-preserved medieval settings of Catalonia. The town was even classified a Historical and Artistic National Monument in 1966. Our guide will explain the importance of the Jewish community that once resided in the city, from the 9th to the 15th century, and share with you the patrimonial inheritance that this community left behind. Discover the most special corners of the city, and get the chance to cross the famous Romanesque bridge of Besalú, which is a must for anyone visiting this medieval city.
content.summary: Start your tour from your hotel, and get picked up by your guide in a comfy minivan to start your tour.

raw.name: Besalú and Medieval Towns - Full-Day Private Tour

options.name: The Best of Antonio Gaudí in Barcelona: Private Full-Day Tour

content.name: The Best of Antonio Gaudí in Barcelona: Private Full-Day Tour
importantInfo: Please wear clothing that conceals the shoulders, legs and arms when visiting religious sites.
content.description:
Explore the best of Barcelona on a private tour of Gaudi’s imprint on the city, with tickets to La Sagrada Familia, Casa Batlló, and Park Güell!

 

On this walking and driving combined private tour, you will:

·      Learn all about Gaudi's work on a private guided tour inside Casa Batlló.

·      Travel along Passeig de Gracia in a private vehicle to Park Güell, with breathtaking views of the city.

·      Stop briefly to see two architectural marvels of Gaudi’s work: Casa Vicens (his first house) and Casa Milá.

·      Walk together with your guide along Park Güell and admire the ceramic animal sculptures and architectural designs that make it so iconic.

·      Take a short comfort break before returning to the vehicle for a private, hassle-free transfer to the Sagrada Familia.

·      Enjoy a one-hour guided tour of La Sagrada Familia, the largest unfinished Catholic Church in the world.

 

A trip to Barcelona would be incomplete without exploring Gaudí's imprint on the city. Not only was he one of the most influential architects of Barcelona’s modern and art nouveau period in the late 19th and early 20th century, but in history. His architectural masterpieces helped transform the city of Barcelona into the thriving, bustling, historical, and cultural metropolitan city it is today.

 

In Dreta de l'Eixample view the world-renowned Casa Batlló, Casa Milá, and Casa Vicens Gaudí. You will get a sense of Gaudí’s innovative and eccentric mind as you walk into the various edifices designed by him, from the cathedral spires at La Sagrada Familia to the gingerbread gatehouses, grottos, and ceramic animals in Park Güell.

 

Enjoy two hassle-free private transfers to Park Güell and Sagrada Familia and admire the city from the comfort of your car. Your tour ends with a guided tour inside the Sagrada Familia, where you will then be free to stay at your own leisure. Join us on the tour, and you will be transformed into another world as you enter the intricately designed spaces.

raw.name: The Best of Antonio Gaudí in Barcelona: Private Full-Day Tour

options.name: The Gothic Quarter: Private Half-Day Tour with Barcelona Cathedral

content.name: The Gothic Quarter: Private Half-Day Tour with Barcelona Cathedral
importantInfo: Please wear clothing that conceals the shoulders, legs and arms when visiting religious sites.
content.description:
Explore Barcelona's iconic Ramblas and the historic Gothic Quarter filled with spectacular architecture in Gothic style, including the remains of the Roman-era city walls, many medieval landmarks and Barcelona Cathedral, famous for its statues of gargoyles and other mythic creatures. Uncover the rich history of the gothic centre of Barcelona, which has developed since the Roman period to become the thriving capital of Catalonia that it is today.

 

On your private half-day tour, you will: 

·      Explore the Gothic Quarter (El Barri Gòtic in Catalonian), one of the most exciting parts of the city as it’s home to some of Barcelona’s most famous attractions.

·      Take a stroll down the world-famous La Rambla promenade.

·      See the remains of the ancient Roman city walls that rose to a full 60 feet and boasted 78 towers.

·      Learn about the stories behind Barcelona landmarks and their long history.

·      Try your luck at haggling in La Boquería marketplace and see where locals shop for their produce and meats.

·      Visit Plaça Reial, a vibrant and beautiful square in the heart of the city, with Gaudi-designed lampposts and an incredible fountain that draws in locals and visitors alike.

·      Walk through the Jewish Quarter of Barcelona, a small but vibrant district with several synagogues and other religious sights that honour the important role of Jewish merchants in medieval Barcelona.

·      Discover sleepy churchyards, medieval alleyways and crumbling Gothic Palaces.

·      Learn about the Roman beginnings of the Old Town and see remnants of this lost past.

·      Finish your tour with a guided visit to the majestic Barcelona Cathedral and its famous rooftop filled with gargoyles and mythical creatures.

 

The Gothic Quarter – also known as Barri Gòtic, is the area that stretches from La Rambla to Via Laietana, and from the Mediterranean seafront to Ronda de Sant Pere and is the centre of the old city of Barcelona. It is known as the Gothic Quarter, as it used to be the location of a Roman village and some of the remnants still remain. 

 

You will visit the Jewish Quarter, known as the Call, a collection of streets in Barcelona’s Gothic Quarter that was established by a group of Jewish merchants in the 13th century. This section of the area was established because this group of people weren’t welcome anywhere else on the mainland along with everyone else, they built their own space to live, build churches or synagogues and freely practice any religion they wanted in peace.

 

Your tour will end with entrance tickets and a guided tour of Barcelona Cathedral. The Cathedral of the Holy Cross and Saint Eulalia is a gorgeous Gothic cathedral in the old centre of the bustling Catalan city; it is also the seat of the Archbishop of Spain. Construction of the Cathedral began at the end of the 13th century during the reign of King James II of Aragon and took around 150 years to complete. While the structure and internal decoration of the Cathedral were completed by the 15th century, its neo-Gothic façade was added at the end of the 19th century, covering the rather plain original exterior; the roof (which your tickets give you access to) was also adorned with an impressive range of gargoyles. 

 

Enjoy this masterpiece as the capstone to your highlights tour!

raw.name: The Gothic Quarter: Private Half-Day Tour with Barcelona Cathedral

options.name: Immersive Audio Walks Barcelona

content.name: Immersive Audio Walks Barcelona
content.description:
Complement your tours with self-guided immersive audio-walks and live the magic of the city. All the surrealism of Dali is presented on a special walking tour in Paseo de Gracia. Travellers can complement their visit into Sagrada Familia with a tour outside of its surroundings. With GPS guidance and special 3D sound effects travellers also discover the legends of the Gothic Neighbourhood in an immersive unforgettable journey. In all three experiences actors perform directly to their headsets.

Discover the history of Paseo de Gracia, Plaza Cataluña, Ateneu Barcelonés, Liceu Theatre, Guell Palace and the surroundings of the Sagrada Familia: plaza de la Sagrada Familia, Cerdeña and Provenza streets, La Marina Street, the Cathedral, Pont del Bisbe, Sant Jaume Square and Santa Maria Square.

How does it work?
To discover Barcelona like never before, don't forget to do download the app on your smart device here.
Select Barcelona, and activate your tour by introducing the code that appears on your voucher, and wait for your audiotour to begin!
What's more, with access to all the available audio walks in Barcelona, interactive GPS maps, and more, your sightseeing experience just got a lot more easier!

raw.name: Immersive Audio Walks Barcelona

options.name: Girona and Costa Brava - Full-Day Tour with Transfers

content.name: Girona and Costa Brava - Full-Day Tour with Transfers
importantInfo: Children must be accompanied by an adult. </br> Please make sure you answer all the mandatory questions during the booking process.
content.description:
Travel through history in Girona and immerse in the pristine scenery of Costa Brava. Experience a unique landscape along the way and head into the historic medieval town of Girona. Enjoy a walking tour of the Old Town, have a look at the widest Gothic Cathedral in the world, and be surprised by the colorful spectacle of the houses along the Onyar River. After the Girona tour, drive to Costa Brava and breathe its unique surroundings and nature.

Highlights
• Marvel at the medieval architecture of Girona, while exploring the city's Jewish Quarter
• Soak up Girona's unique atmosphere by walking through its winding streets
• Visit a 19th-century iron bridge designed by Gustave Eiffel
• Get to see the most breathtaking views of the Costa Brava
• Visit the historical town of Pals and the fisherman’s village of Calella de Palafrugell
• A small group tour of maximum 8 people to ensure a better experience and more personal attention led by an expert guide

Start your tour with a comfortable pick-up at your accommodation in Barcelona to head directly to the historic town of Girona, where the remains of a Roman and medieval past coexist harmoniously with the present, offering a complex image full of contrasts. Get lost in the maze of narrow streets and travel through time in the best-preserved Jewish neighborhood in Europe with your professional guide.

Admire the Cathedral, boasting the widest Gothic nave in the world, and marvel at the colorful spectacle of houses along Onyar River, which splits the city in two. Because of its beautiful and historic scenery, the medieval center was chosen for some of the most important scenes in the popular series Game of Thrones. Whether by its many bridges crisscrossing the river or by its strong medieval walls, Girona charms its visitors with an exciting panorama.

After exploring Girona with your guide, we will drive to Costa Brava, the northern side of Catalonia’s sunny Mediterranean coast. Before delving completely into nature, we will stop at the picturesque village of Pals. This ancient medieval village was once a fortress, still featuring idyllic cobbled streets and picture-perfect plazas. Its towers and other viewpoints offer the best views over the Mediterranean Sea and will allow you to see as far as the fields and Empordà plains extending into the horizon.

Next, we will continue to the Catalan coast, where you will enjoy the most breathtaking views: green mountains, the sheer relief of the coast, and small villages with whitewashed houses built around the swell of the Costa Brava. We will stop in Calella de Palafrugell, a traditional fishing village that fully retains the charm of its past. Before exploring the surrounding area, we will take a moment to relax and taste some of the delicious local cuisines.

The Caminos de Ronda (ancient pathways), which you will observe near Calella, lead visitors to discover the most pristine corners of the region. Fishermen, watchmen, and smugglers once used these pathways, that allow you to explore the coastal scenery. Follow the pathways to uncover the most popular beaches in the area, as well as hidden coves and cliffsides. You can also take a dip in the crystal clear water, surrounded by unspoiled nature.

The perfect Catalan tour for your family and friends!

raw.name: Girona and Costa Brava - Full-Day Tour with Transfers

options.name: Girona and Dali Museum in Figueres - Tour with Transfers

content.name: Girona and Dali Museum in Figueres - Tour with Transfers
importantInfo: Children must be accompanied by an adult. </br> Please make sure you answer all the mandatory questions during the booking process.
content.description:
Leave the hustle and bustle of Barcelona behind and travel back in time to discover the medieval city of Girona. Walk through the defensive walls of the city to enjoy great bird’s eye views of the surrounding area. Visit the best-preserved Jewish Quarter of Europe and marvel at the Gothic Cathedral, famous for having the world’s widest Gothic nave. Continue to Dali’s hometown, travel to Figueres to visit the Dali Theatre Museum, the largest surrealist object in the world and a present that Dali himself wanted to leave to his city.

Highlights
• Marvel at the medieval architecture of Girona, while exploring the city's Jewish Quarter
• Soak up Girona's unique atmosphere by walking through its winding streets
• Visit a 19th-century iron bridge designed by Gustave Eiffel
• Explore Figueres, the artist's birthplace and current home of the Dalí Museum
• Tour the Dalí Museum in Figueres, dedicated to the city's most famous creative genius
• Get an inside look at the life of Salvador Dalí during a guided tour of his former residence
• Learn about the formative experiences that inspired and shaped Dali’s work

After morning pickup at your Barcelona accommodation, travel with your guide for about 1 hour to reach the colorful city of Girona. During the drive, learn about Girona’s long and rich history, from its ancient Roman roots through a thriving medieval era and evolution into a hip, modern Spanish city.

When you arrive, explore Old Town on a walking tour that takes you through past landmarks like the Girona Cathedral and an iron bridge designed by famous French engineer Gustave Eiffel.

Get a sense of life in medieval Spain while wandering through the city’s labyrinthine Jewish quarter, and rest your feet over a cup of coffee in a charming Old Town cafe. With your expert guide continue your tour to the small town of Figueres, located near the French border. Best known as the birthplace of surrealist artist Salvador Dalí, Figueres honors the legacy of its famous former resident with the Dalí Museum — designed by the artist himself and considered the world’s largest surrealist object.

Explore Dalí’s life and work through the museum’s collection of his dimension-bending paintings and other original artifacts.

Relax on the return drive through the countryside to Barcelona, where the 10-hour tour concludes with drop-off at your original departure point in the evening.

raw.name: Girona and Dali Museum in Figueres - Tour with Transfers
